reviews.review_msg: Prof. Linley is super kind. She gives a ton of feedback on assignments, although takes a long time to get back. I found her lectures vary draining and found it easier to learn the difficult and dense material on my own. It wad also a bit annoying that we would consistently go over time for class but overall assignments are easy and she is great!

Kind, welcoming, and always open to questions. Would recommend staying after class to go over questions or potential ideas for assignments, because she really puts in the effort to help you understand. Lectures were confusing at times, but discussions with her always helped clear them up.
One of my favourite profs at SFU

This prof is great! If you do the readings, participate in class once and a while, and do the assigned work, you will do well. Lectures can be a bit slow at times but she is caring and helpful. I'd recommend her classes but be ready to write proper English papers and do the readings.

Lots of reading for this class, often reading entire novels to only discuss them for one week. However, Margaret always had interesting supplemental articles to go with the books/poetry that enhanced the learning experience. Very discussion heavy, but she didn't force people to contribute if they weren't comfortable doing so.

First of all, Margaret is an insanely caring prof who wants to see her students succeed. She was very accessible when I reached out to her outside of class and she had very clear marking criteria. The readings were heavy at times, but expected for an upper div course. Overall, Margaret is a great prof and if you put in the effort you will do great!

This is the second English course I have taken at SFU and let me just say this prof was amazing. I truly enjoyed her lectures, as they were not excessively long and they were straight to the point. Also, she was extremely helpful through email if you ever had any questions.

I won't speak too much about her teaching style or how much she cares. This was an asynchronous remote class, I never once talked to her. I will say the materials in her class introduced me to some amazaing literature, some classic, some contemporary. And she really awakened a whole new perspective on nature, literatue and culture.

Dr. Linley goes out of her way to help students understand if they ask questions. She was super accessible outside of class and seemed like she really wanted people to succeed. The readings were pretty dense but she would give lots of information during lectures to help make the material more digestible.
Her lectures are completely off topic to what you will be graded on. No aspects of writing are given, she just summarizes the books you are already going to read.

Margaret is clearly knowledgeable about and interested in the subject matter, and is always open to questions and discussion in class. Her lectures were not always enthralling but her slides did highlight the information you needed to know for the final exam. Not a hard class in terms of what you are learning but the marking was usually harsh.

Lectures are boring and the slides are very scattered. Linley struggles to keep on topic and to use lecture time to address significance of the works we read. She spends a lot of unnecessary time going over author biographies and wastes class time.
Margaret is really interested in the subject matter, is very fair when grading essays and presentations, and gives good feedback on assignments. Would definitely take another class from her!

Margaret is a great prof. You can see that she is really passionate about what she teaches and takes the time to make sure you understand the subject matter. Her grading is fair and she always encourages students to come see her about improving their grades. I highly recommend taking a class with her.

The prof & the TA I had for this class really helped me improve my writing style. My first essay gave me a C- in the course; I went into office hours to get advice on what I could have done better. My TA was a big help for that, but immediately in lecture the prof covered common problems that everyone had in the essay. Final was based on lectures.

Class attendance is essentially a waste of time, the lectures aren't important or related to what you are graded on. Class attendance dropped to half the original number, a week into the semester. Basically people only attend class if they're around, bored or have nothing better to do. Actual course is simple, books are boring though.

She is an amazing professor. She goes in depth of the lecture material and beyond what is on her slides. She's always open for discussion and is extremely kind. Her love and passion for her job make an amazing professor. Engl112 was a great course because she was super engaging and helpful

She's nice and passionate about what she teaches. The lectures were a bit boring at times, but that did not stop the class from being engaged in learning. She knows a ton about Victorian literature, and the final paper was the paper I enjoyed writing the most in my university years.

She is a very nice lady, but this class was extremely boring. 4 hours and she gives a 15-20 min break. Readings are extremely long and very boring. She's a pretty tough marker too, I usually get A's/B+ and ended up with a low B. Super kind lady though, I  just feel like the content of the course was extremely boring.

She seems very passionate about what she's teaching and tries her best to stimulate a class discussion.  If you ask a question, she is very adamant of answering your question as best as she can, (Students often would ask extremely complex questions and she will keep going through it with you until your question is answered to your satisfaction.)

IM DYING FROM BOREDOM IN THIS CLASS. The material is dry and the 4 hour class is dryer. I gave up reading after the first week. The grading for paper was super tough, and I only show up for class because I have to (you lose grades if you don't show up). I can't wait til this semester is over. Also she doesn't give hourly breaks, only 10 minutes.
This prof is amazing. I highly recommend taking her 19c lit classes.

Issues in Literature and Culture: study of Frankenstein. Wonderful course! Took it in my first semester at SFU. This class sealed the deal for my degree choice. Linley is a wonderful prof that is incredibly passionate about her material. Needless to mention, teaching first year courses are a bit of a challenge for profs- but she pulls it off well.

Although knowledgable, Dr. Linley is an extremely unengaging lecturer. Her lecture slides were disorganized, confusing, and impossible to study from. The tutorials were much more valuable than the lecture. Furthermore, the amount of readings required were too much for a 100-level course.  I do not recommend taking Engl 105 with Dr. Linley.

The class has way too much reading! Novels are not bad, some were even good, but the short stories are horrible and so boring you can't even read them. The powerpoint notes she proves are pretty confusing and random. Couple TAs were also pretty hard markers.

Compared to the other 100 level English courses, this course had a ridiculous amount of reading. I felt the material was very dull and bland, and perhaps the prof was just not used to teaching a lower level English course. I fear for the worst for the final.

Dr Linley's lectures were unengaging, and her lecture slides confusing and hard to follow. She was extremely disorganized - one of the required texts she had assigned were incompatible with macbooks. The amount of readings required was too much for a 100 level course.

An excellent teacher with an incredible level of knowledge on the subject of Romantic and Victorian literature. Only complaint is the breadth exceeds what can be retained. If you're looking for a prof that takes their subject and your comprehension and critical appraisal of it seriously, look no further. Excellent prof.

She is very, very knowledgeable about a range of critical theory and literature, and also very approachable. She does not manage discussion proactively, so often the same people talk over and over for too long.

Professor Linley is a genius. She is approachable and helpful and wants to see you succeed. Her assignments are fair and fun. She knows her theory like nobody's business. I think she's the best teacher I've ever had at SFU. And I'm in my fifth year.

Dr. Linley is articulate and intelligent. Expect many readings per week if you're in her theory course. I found that the material we studied is useful for other courses that invoke themes like culture, gender, and language.

Margaret is a very intelligent woman who has a passion for literature. She has some very unique insights. The reading in this class was insane! She's really approachable, and I got the feeling that she really wanted to help me succeed. I wish we had spent more time on the application of literary criticism, as I found the discussions often digressed

She is obviously very passionate about the subject matter and very knowledgeable, but she does not seem to have any sense of clarity when explaining the material. Hardly anyone talks in the class because her questions are so convoluted. She is open to answering questions; however her answers are often just as complicated as what she said originally
Really interesting course. Don't expect an easy A. I found Margaret really friendly and approachable, and her lectures made sense of texts that often read like gobbledegook
Best Prof ever!
A very knowledgeable and approachable professor.

Not very personable...doesn't seem to bother remembering names, etc. Makes interesting texts seem dull. Went from A+ average on upper level english papers to a B in her class...which seemed to be her favourite grade, she gave it to everyone I talked to. Avoid her.
She is somewhat of a fair marker.  She does focus on the biographies of the authors way too much.  Her lectures could be a bit confusing since she's not very clear.  Overall, she's an OK professor

Very fair & kind marker,but needs to work on clarity during lectures.Focuses on authors' biographies way too much.She really cares about how the works related to the peroids in which they were written-v. historical approach-everything should be analyzed as a document of its time. Doesn't like to provide examples of points to be made using texts.
Great prof! really enjoyed her lectures

Great course. A little inconsistent in terms of reading load - some weeks were very light and some were killer.  Very supportive and attentive.  Interesting, well constructed course.  Good balance of literature, theory, and other media.
She seems to be very busy...thus, she can be disorganized and too busy to notice you. Picky marker, likes to lecture.  Not the best but not the worst~ c'est la vie
...could be better!

She is a great prof... she was witty and clear.Always available for extra help, really good for eager upper division students and not for those pompus lower level students who think they're intellegent, and obiously are't...she'll see right through you
exasperating lectures--needs to be discriminating instead of encouraging moronic thought from all the eager beaver pseudo-intellectuals. unapproachable outside of class.
Each lecture is like she's reading an essay.  I like the way she gets the class involved but its mostly boring.  I wouldn't recommend her.
Her lectures were so boring as she just read directly from the notepad. Get on her good side so she will talk to you or you will be ignored for the semester.
A great prof for upper-levels students who want to challenge themselves and really get into literary criticism.  Very approachable.
BORING
No Comments
if ya didn't like her, ya didn't get it.
A teacher who doesn't know how to pronounce  Onomatopoeia  correctly shouldn't be teaching poetry. Very disorganized
Helpful and well learned...but...isn't what you would call enthusiastic...bring a pillow if English isn't your thing.
She is great and really knows her stuff which.  Very interested in content!
Great prof.  Interesting and stimulating to creative thought.
Great prof, very intelligent, always challenges her students.
Extremely intelligent and engaging, but yes she's a toughie, will challenge you
worst prof ever for english...reads from printed notes and can even messes it up
No Comments
No Comments
very well versed, she knows her stuff, but that can be intimidating
if you are the type of person who likes to sleep in class, take this course
great prof for upperlevels students
I'm still confused on what I have learned so far... or have I learned anything&#
Intelligent and Insightful..lectures r focused on what WE think
she is boring and can't pronounce poetry terms right
